site: Knossos (Western Magazine IX, fifth pithos from the north-west corner)
certainty: No syllabic sequence is printed: each of the four instances is the single sign AB 131a, printed with no dots and no brackets. SCRIPT ATTRIBUTION UNCERTAIN — verbatim (p. 99): "Etant donné que A 131a et VIN sont homomorphes, il n'est pas exclu, donc, qu'il puisse s'agir d'une inscription en linéaire B." (Brice dates the pithos LM I, Christakis assigns it LM IIIA — hence the Linear B possibility, though incised Linear B vase inscriptions are "rarissimes", cf. KH Z 16 and DI Z 1, fn. 65.) A second uncertainty, on the identity of Boskamp's third sign with Brice II 6iii / KN Zb <38> of GORILA IV: "Cela reste toutefois incertain", since Brice assigns the first two to Magazine IX but the third only generically to the "Palace Magazines".
source: Del Freo, Rapport 2016-2021, KO-RO-NO-WE-SA (Ariadne Suppl. 5), pp. 98-99 (sha 40486242...); primary: GORILA IV (KN Zb <36>, <37>, after Evans' drawings in Brice 1961, 15, no. II 6ii, Pl. XXIII); Boskamp 1996, 101-112 (KN 068, Pl. 25b); Christakis 2005, 60; renumbering per Firth 2016, Minos 39, 229-247 (at 247)
notes: words is empty because the inscription consists solely of the logogram-homomorph sign AB 131a (Linear A *131a / VIN homomorph), four instances on ONE pithos — logograms are kept out of words by convention. History: KN Zb <36> and Zb <37> (both AB 131a), published in GORILA IV from Evans' drawings reproduced in Brice, were located by Anton Boskamp in 1991-92 on the fifth pithos from the NW corner of Western Magazine IX; Boskamp also spotted a third AB 131a on the same pithos (proposed = another Brice number, corresponding to KN Zb <38> of GORILA IV — uncertain, see certainty), and Kostis Christakis, re-examining the pithos, identified a fourth that had escaped Boskamp. Following Firth's observation that <36> and <37> sit on the same vase, <36> becomes 36a and <37> 36b (analogue: HT Zb 158a/158b); the Boskamp and Christakis signs become 36c and 36d. This entry is thus a re-identification/renumbering of GORILA items plus two new instances, not a wholly new text. Per fn. 61, Boskamp 1996, 105-108 would also see the wine logogram incised on pithoi KN 060, KN 070 (Magazine IX) and KN 084 (Magazine XI), but "Les tracés reproduits par l'auteur suscitent toutefois des doutes" (cf. Hallager 2002, 64).
